problem.title: Erdos Problem #4
problem.statement: Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\]

subgoals: Milestone 1: reduction from prime-free interval to p_{n+1}-p_n bound
Milestone 2: valid CRT covering lemma with pairwise-coprime structure
Milestone 3: parameter-growth lemma linking N, Q, and n
Is it true that, for any $C>0$, there are infinitely many $n$ such that\[p_{n+1}-p_n> C\frac{\log\log n\log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log \log n)^2}\log n?\]

formalize: â constants c1, c2 >0 such that for arbitrarily large k, there exist distinct primes q1<...<q_r â¤ k log k, exponents e_i â¥1 with m_i = q_i^{e_i} pairwise coprime, Q=â m_i â¤ exp(c1 k), and residues r_i mod m_i s.t. âª_i {d â [1, L] : d â¡ r_i mod m_i} = [1,L] with L â¥ c2 (log Q log log Q log log log log Q) / (log log log Q)^2.

formalize: If there are infinitely many prime-free intervals (N, N + L] with N â¥ 1, L > 2 C \frac{\log N \log\log N \log\log\log\log N}{(\log\log\log N)^2}, then for n = \pi(N), we have p_{n+1} - p_n > C \frac{\log n \log\log n \log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log\log n)^2} \log n for infinitely many n, using li(N) bounds.

moves.claim: â constants c1, c2 >0 such that for arbitrarily large k, there exist distinct primes q1<...<q_r â¤ k log k, exponents e_i â¥1 with m_i = q_i^{e_i} pairwise coprime, Q=â m_i â¤ exp(c1 k), and residues r_i mod m_i s.t. âª_i {d â [1, L] : d â¡ r_i mod m_i} = [1,L] with L â¥ c2 (log Q log log Q log log log log Q) / (log log log Q)^2.
moves.rationale: Standard ErdÅs-Rankin covering construction using prime powers for distinct primes; quantitative bounds from greedy covering or known tables tuned to match target growth.
moves.test_plan: Implement small k=5,10 in Lean/Python: find explicit m_i, r_i covering [1,L]; scale to verify growth; if Lean times out, fallback to existence via combinatorial argument.
dependencies: Existence of covering systems with distinct prime power moduli
moves.expected_effect: Provides the CRT covering for prime-free intervals of target length, advancing to parameter linking.
moves.why_this_applies_here: Directly matches milestone 2; pairwise-coprime prime powers standard for CRT solvability without conflicts.
required_preconditions: Uncertain: exact constants c1,c2; assumes known covering densities
moves.how_to_falsify_fast: For k=10, compute max L coverable with râ¤10 prime powers â¤ exp(10); if L < 10 log( exp(10) ) say, falsify growth.

moves.claim: If there are infinitely many prime-free intervals (N, N + L] with N â¥ 1, L > 2 C \frac{\log N \log\log N \log\log\log\log N}{(\log\log\log N)^2}, then for n = \pi(N), we have p_{n+1} - p_n > C \frac{\log n \log\log n \log\log\log\log n}{(\log\log\log n)^2} \log n for infinitely many n, using li(N) bounds.
moves.rationale: Standard transfer: p_n â¤ N < p_{n+1}, so gap > L; PNT gives \log n â¼ \log N, matching log-scale terms asymptotically for large N.
moves.test_plan: Symbolically verify inequality L > f(n) using \pi(N) â¼ N / \log N and change of variables; Lean tactic with real_asymp or logssimp.
moves.expected_effect: Completes Milestone 1, reduces main goal to prime-free intervals.
moves.why_this_applies_here: Direct analog to ErdÅs-Rankin reductions; log terms match problem exactly up to constants.
moves.how_to_falsify_fast: Check if \log n = \log \pi(N) â¼ \log(N / \log N) = \log N + o(1) preserves the iterated log ratios; numeric eval for N=exp(exp(10)).

evaluations.reason: Mapped Lean theorem is tautological; formal progress not credited.
